items.title: Training loss vs evaluation metric
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  trainNode["train split"]
  fitNode["fit model minimize MSE or MAE loss"]
  testNode["test split"]
  metricNode["report RMSE and/or MAE"]
  lossTopic["Loss Functions topic"]
  metricTopic["RMSE and MAE topic"]
  trainNode --> fitNode
  fitNode --> lossTopic
  testNode --> metricNode
  metricNode --> metricTopic
items.caption: Loss Functions owns the training objective; this topic owns metrics computed on held-out data after fit.

items.title: RMSE from squared residuals
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  pairsNode["n pairs y_i y_hat_i"]
  resNode["residuals e_i = y_i - y_hat_i"]
  sqNode["square e_i squared"]
  meanNode["average to MSE"]
  sqrtNode["square root to RMSE"]
  pairsNode --> resNode
  resNode --> sqNode
  sqNode --> meanNode
  meanNode --> sqrtNode
items.caption: RMSE is the square root of mean squared residual — same core formula as MSE loss, different reporting role.

items.title: Choosing RMSE vs MAE for reporting
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  startNode["regression model on test set"]
  outlierNode["large errors especially costly?"]
  skewNode["heavy tails or outlier labels?"]
  rmseNode["report RMSE"]
  maeNode["report MAE"]
  bothNode["report both for full picture"]
  startNode --> outlierNode
  outlierNode -->|yes| rmseNode
  outlierNode -->|no| skewNode
  skewNode -->|yes| maeNode
  skewNode -->|unclear| bothNode
items.caption: RMSE emphasizes large misses; MAE gives a robust typical-error readout in target units.
